intent: Write a short 'behind the headline' piece on a current /f/worldnews topic with Wikipedia context. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) Reddit — browse /f/worldnews AND /f/news for posts on a single concrete current-events topic (pick one with multiple posts). Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, comment count, and representative comments. (2) Wikipedia — open the article that provides background (country, organization, historical event) and extract 3-4 facts. The report must: (a) name the story and its Wikipedia background, (b) summarize the community reaction, (c) point out one fact from Wikipedia that complicates the dominant Reddit framing, (d) close with a neutral one-paragraph summary. Cite every claim. Do not fabricate news events.
author_notes: Easy tier. Reddit (worldnews + news) + Wikipedia. News-with-context pattern.
